title: Baeckea corynophylla
intro: Baeckea corynophylla, also known as the coastal baeckea, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The coastal baeckea has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Baeckea corynophylla include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with coastal baeckea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
